news_summary: Today's environmental progress highlights advancements in renewable energy, innovative sustainable farming practices, significant reductions in Amazon deforestation, and creative clean transportation solutions amid fuel shortages.
news_summary_fr: Les progrès environnementaux enregistrés aujourd'hui mettent en avant les avancées dans le domaine des énergies renouvelables, les pratiques agricoles durables et innovantes, la réduction significative de la déforestation en Amazonie, ainsi que les solutions créatives en matière de transports propres face aux pénuries de carburant.
news_summary_es: Los avances medioambientales de hoy en día destacan los progresos en el ámbito de las energías renovables, las prácticas agrícolas sostenibles e innovadoras, las reducciones significativas de la deforestación en la Amazonía y las soluciones creativas de transporte limpio ante la escasez de combustible.

articles.title: UK's largest wind farm to be rebuilt with fewer, larger turbines
articles.summary: Whitelee Windfarm has been producing electricity for about 20 years on a stretch of moorland near Eaglesham but is reaching the end of its life.
articles.content:
One of Europe's biggest onshore wind farms is to be flattened and rebuilt with fewer, taller turbines, its owner has announced.

Whitelee Windfarm has been producing electricity for about 20 years on a stretch of moorland near Eaglesham, south of Glasgow, but is reaching the end of its life.

Scottish Power Renewables says the existing 215 turbines will be replaced in three phases with around 124, each of which will be about 110m (361ft) taller.

The £1.5 billion "repowering" project will take 10 years to complete and will double the electricity output from 539 megawatts to 1 gigawatt, which is enough to power about 650,000 homes.

An application has been made to the Scottish government's energy consents unit and the full planning application is expected to be submitted by the end of next year.

It is anticipated that the upgrade will be fully operational by 2035.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant renewable energy project that will double electricity output from a major wind farm, benefiting a large number of homes and contributing to clean energy goals. The project has broad environmental and societal impact, with detailed plans and a clear positive outcome.
articles.personality_title: UK’s largest wind farm to double power with taller turbines
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Whitelee Windfarm near Eaglesham, south of Glasgow, has been generating electricity for about 20 years. It is one of Europe’s biggest onshore wind farms but is now reaching the end of its working life.

**What happened** – The owner, Scottish Power Renewables, announced a plan to replace the 215 existing turbines with around 124 new, taller turbines. These turbines will be about 110 meters taller than the current ones. The project will cost £1.5 billion and take about 10 years to finish.

**Impact** – This upgrade will double the wind farm’s electricity output from 539 megawatts to 1 gigawatt. That amount of power can supply around 650,000 homes, making clean energy available to many more people. Using fewer but larger turbines will also improve efficiency.

**What’s next step** – Scottish Power Renewables has applied to the Scottish government for permission to start the project. They expect to submit the full plan by the end of next year. The new wind farm should be fully working by 2035.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The Whitelee Windfarm upgrade will provide twice as much clean energy to hundreds of thousands of homes by replacing old turbines with fewer, taller ones over the next decade.
articles.personality_title_fr: La plus grande ferme éolienne du Royaume-Uni doublera sa production avec des turbines plus hautes
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – La ferme éolienne de Whitelee, près d’Eaglesham au sud de Glasgow, produit de l’électricité depuis environ 20 ans. C’est l’une des plus grandes fermes éoliennes terrestres d’Europe, mais elle arrive en fin de vie.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Le propriétaire, Scottish Power Renewables, a annoncé un projet pour remplacer les 215 turbines actuelles par environ 124 turbines plus hautes. Ces nouvelles turbines seront environ 110 mètres plus grandes que les actuelles. Le projet coûtera 1,5 milliard de livres et prendra environ 10 ans.

**Impact** – Cette rénovation doublera la production d’électricité de la ferme, passant de 539 mégawatts à 1 gigawatt. Cette énergie peut alimenter environ 650 000 foyers, rendant l’énergie propre accessible à beaucoup plus de personnes. Utiliser moins de turbines mais plus grandes améliorera aussi l’efficacité.

**Prochaine étape** – Scottish Power Renewables a demandé l’autorisation au gouvernement écossais pour démarrer le projet. Le plan complet devrait être soumis d’ici la fin de l’année prochaine. La nouvelle ferme devrait être pleinement opérationnelle d’ici 2035.

**Une phrase clé** – La rénovation de la ferme éolienne de Whitelee fournira deux fois plus d’énergie propre à des centaines de milliers de foyers en remplaçant les anciennes turbines par des turbines plus hautes dans la prochaine décennie.
articles.personality_title_es: La mayor granja eólica del Reino Unido duplicará su energía con turbinas más altas
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – La granja eólica Whitelee, cerca de Eaglesham al sur de Glasgow, ha estado generando electricidad durante unos 20 años. Es una de las mayores granjas eólicas terrestres de Europa, pero está llegando al final de su vida útil.

**Qué pasó** – El propietario, Scottish Power Renewables, anunció un plan para reemplazar las 215 turbinas actuales con alrededor de 124 turbinas nuevas y más altas. Estas turbinas serán unos 110 metros más altas que las actuales. El proyecto costará 1.500 millones de libras y tomará unos 10 años.

**Impacto** – Esta actualización duplicará la producción eléctrica de la granja, pasando de 539 megavatios a 1 gigavatio. Esta cantidad de energía puede alimentar a unas 650,000 casas, haciendo que la energía limpia llegue a muchas más personas. Usar menos turbinas pero más grandes también mejorará la eficiencia.

**Próximo paso** – Scottish Power Renewables ha solicitado permiso al gobierno escocés para iniciar el proyecto. Esperan presentar el plan completo para finales del próximo año. La nueva granja debería estar completamente operativa para 2035.

**Una frase clave** – La renovación de la granja eólica Whitelee proporcionará el doble de energía limpia a cientos de miles de hogares al reemplazar las turbinas viejas por otras más altas en la próxima década.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of a sprawling moorland near Glasgow at sunrise, featuring a mix of slender, towering wind turbines gracefully rising above the landscape, with some shorter, older turbines fading into the background, symbolizing renewal and progress in clean energy. The scene uses soft natural earth tones and gentle sky colors to evoke hope and sustainable growth.

articles.title: How farmers could fix Wales' polluted rivers and save money
articles.summary: Farmers have a crucial role in cleaning up one of the UK's most polluted river systems, a study shows.
articles.content:
Farmers have a crucial role in cleaning up one of the UK's most polluted river systems and could save money in the process, a new study has shown.

The eastern and western Cleddau rivers in Pembrokeshire flow through farmland which produces as much as 25% of Wales' milk.

Researchers from Lancaster University found the area's farms were buying in far more fertiliser and processed animal feed than needed, causing a build-up of phosphorus in the soil and polluting waterways.

Now - in what's thought to be one of the largest projects of its kind in the world - hundreds of dairy farms are working out their "nutrient footprint" to help tackle the problem.
articles.reasoning: The article reports on a large-scale, impactful environmental initiative where farmers in Wales are actively working to reduce pollution in a heavily polluted river system by managing nutrient use more efficiently, which benefits water quality and could save money. This represents significant progress in conservation with broad societal and ecological benefits.
articles.personality_title: Farmers in Wales work together to clean polluted rivers and cut costs
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – The eastern and western Cleddau rivers in Pembrokeshire, Wales, are among the most polluted river systems in the UK. These rivers run through farmland that produces about a quarter of Wales' milk.

**What happened** – Researchers from Lancaster University discovered that many farms in this area use more fertiliser and animal feed than necessary. This extra use causes phosphorus to build up in the soil, which then pollutes the rivers. To fix this, hundreds of dairy farms have started calculating their "nutrient footprint"—a way to measure how much fertiliser and feed they use—to reduce pollution.

**Impact** – This project is one of the largest of its kind worldwide. By using fertilisers and feed more carefully, farmers can improve water quality in the rivers and save money by using fewer resources. This shows how farming practices can change to help the environment without hurting farm income.

**What's next step** – The participating farms will continue to track and reduce their nutrient use. If successful, this approach could be shared with other farming areas facing pollution problems, leading to cleaner rivers across the country.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Welsh farmers are reducing pollution in key rivers by managing fertiliser and feed better, improving water quality and saving money at the same time.
articles.personality_title_fr: Les agriculteurs du Pays de Galles unissent leurs efforts pour nettoyer les rivières polluées et réduire les coûts
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les rivières Cleddau Est et Ouest à Pembrokeshire, au Pays de Galles, sont parmi les systèmes fluviaux les plus pollués du Royaume-Uni. Ces rivières traversent des terres agricoles qui produisent environ un quart du lait gallois.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Des chercheurs de l'Université de Lancaster ont découvert que de nombreuses fermes utilisent plus d'engrais et d'aliments pour animaux que nécessaire. Cet excès provoque une accumulation de phosphore dans le sol, ce qui pollue les rivières. Pour remédier à cela, des centaines de fermes laitières ont commencé à calculer leur "empreinte nutrimentaire" – une mesure de leur utilisation d'engrais et d'aliments – afin de réduire la pollution.

**Impact** – Ce projet est l'un des plus grands de ce type dans le monde. En utilisant les engrais et aliments de façon plus raisonnée, les agriculteurs peuvent améliorer la qualité de l'eau des rivières et économiser de l'argent en réduisant leurs dépenses. Cela montre que les pratiques agricoles peuvent évoluer pour protéger l'environnement sans nuire aux revenus.

**Prochaine étape** – Les fermes participantes continueront à surveiller et diminuer leur utilisation de nutriments. Si cela fonctionne, cette méthode pourra être appliquée dans d'autres régions agricoles confrontées à la pollution, pour des rivières plus propres à travers le pays.

**Phrase clé** – Les agriculteurs gallois réduisent la pollution des rivières en gérant mieux leurs engrais et aliments, améliorant la qualité de l'eau tout en économisant de l'argent.
articles.personality_title_es: Agricultores de Gales trabajan juntos para limpiar ríos contaminados y ahorrar dinero
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los ríos Cleddau Este y Oeste en Pembrokeshire, Gales, están entre los sistemas fluviales más contaminados del Reino Unido. Estos ríos atraviesan tierras agrícolas que producen alrededor del 25% de la leche de Gales.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de la Universidad de Lancaster encontraron que muchas granjas usan más fertilizantes y alimento para animales de lo necesario. Este exceso causa acumulación de fósforo en el suelo, lo que contamina los ríos. Para solucionarlo, cientos de granjas lecheras han comenzado a calcular su "huella de nutrientes" — una forma de medir cuánto fertilizante y alimento usan — para reducir la contaminación.

**Impacto** – Este proyecto es uno de los más grandes de su tipo en el mundo. Al usar fertilizantes y alimento de manera más cuidadosa, los agricultores pueden mejorar la calidad del agua en los ríos y ahorrar dinero al usar menos recursos. Esto demuestra que las prácticas agrícolas pueden cambiar para ayudar al medio ambiente sin afectar los ingresos.

**Próximo paso** – Las granjas participantes seguirán controlando y reduciendo su uso de nutrientes. Si tiene éxito, este método podría aplicarse en otras áreas agrícolas con problemas de contaminación, logrando ríos más limpios en todo el país.

**Frase clave** – Los agricultores galeses están reduciendo la contaminación de ríos clave al manejar mejor fertilizantes y alimento, mejorando la calidad del agua y ahorrando dinero al mismo tiempo.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm painting of a gently flowing river winding through lush Welsh farmland dotted with grazing dairy cows, with subtle visual hints of nutrient cycles—like soft, glowing soil layers beneath the fields and faint, symbolic ripples of clean water—capturing the harmony between responsible farming and river restoration using natural, simple earth tones.

articles.title: Brazil's Amazon sees sharp decline in size of area burned in 2025
articles.summary: The amount of land burned in the world's largest rainforest was the smallest since MapBiomas records began in 1985.
articles.content:
In 2024, the Amazon rainforest experienced its worst forest fires in over two decades, which the Copernicus Climate Change Service put down to "climate extremes and human activity".

"The 2025 figures are significant and show that it is possible to reduce the burned area in the country," Vera Arruda, a researcher at the Amazon Environmental Research Institute (IPAM) and co-ordinator at MapBiomas, told the BBC.

"The 31% drop compared to the historical average - driven primarily by the lowest number of fires recorded in the Amazon in 41 years - is positive news, but it does not yet allow us to conclude that this is an established trend or to scale back prevention efforts," Arruda added.

The MapBiomas report shows a dramatic drop in area burned not just in the Amazon, but across the whole of Brazil in 2025.

Figures show around 12.7 million hectares burned in Brazil in 2025, compared to 30 million hectares in 2024.

The highest number of hectares burned in Brazil since records began was in 2007, when 30.7 million were recorded across the country.

The decline in the 2025 figures partly reflects an easing of the unprecedented fires of 2024, helped by cooler La Niña conditions instead of the warmer El Niño.

But MapBiomas warned the natural weather pattern of El Niño, which pushes up global temperatures, could extend this year's dry season and increase the risk of forest fires.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant and measurable reduction in the area burned in the Amazon rainforest in 2025, marking the smallest burned area since records began in 1985. This represents a positive environmental development with broad implications for climate action and conservation. The story provides detailed context about the scale of the decline, the contributing factors, and cautions about future risks, making it a substantive and meaningful environmental update.
articles.personality_title: Amazon rainforest fires drop to lowest level since 1985 in 2025
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – The Amazon rainforest, the world’s largest tropical forest, suffered its worst fires in over 20 years in 2024. These fires were caused by a mix of extreme weather and human actions, leading to huge areas of land being burned.

**What happened** – In 2025, the area burned in the Amazon fell sharply to the smallest amount recorded since 1985, according to MapBiomas, a group that tracks land use in Brazil. About 12.7 million hectares burned in 2025, compared to 30 million hectares in 2024. This 31% drop was helped by cooler weather conditions known as La Niña.

**Impact** – This is an important change because it shows that it is possible to reduce fires in the Amazon, which is a key part of the Earth’s climate system. Fewer fires mean less damage to wildlife, plants, and the people who live there. However, experts warn that this improvement might not last, especially if warmer weather patterns return.

**What's next step** – Scientists and environmental groups will keep watching the Amazon closely to see if this drop in fires continues. They also want to keep up efforts to prevent fires, especially since weather patterns like El Niño could bring hotter, drier conditions that increase fire risks.

**One-sentence takeaway** – In 2025, the Amazon rainforest saw the smallest area burned in 40 years, showing progress in fighting fires but highlighting the need for continued care and monitoring.
articles.personality_title_fr: Les incendies en Amazonie tombent à leur plus bas niveau depuis 1985 en 2025
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – La forêt amazonienne, la plus grande forêt tropicale du monde, a subi ses pires incendies depuis plus de 20 ans en 2024. Ces incendies étaient causés par un mélange de conditions météorologiques extrêmes et d’activités humaines, brûlant de vastes zones.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – En 2025, la surface brûlée en Amazonie a fortement chuté pour atteindre le plus bas niveau enregistré depuis 1985, selon MapBiomas, un groupe qui suit l’utilisation des terres au Brésil. Environ 12,7 millions d’hectares ont brûlé en 2025, contre 30 millions en 2024. Cette baisse de 31 % a été aidée par des conditions météorologiques plus fraîches appelées La Niña.

**Impact** – Ce changement est important car il montre qu’il est possible de réduire les incendies en Amazonie, qui joue un rôle clé dans le climat mondial. Moins d’incendies signifie moins de dégâts pour la faune, les plantes et les populations locales. Cependant, les experts préviennent que cette amélioration pourrait ne pas durer, surtout si des conditions plus chaudes reviennent.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques et les groupes environnementaux continueront de surveiller l’Amazonie pour voir si cette baisse des incendies se poursuit. Ils veulent aussi maintenir les efforts de prévention, car des phénomènes comme El Niño pourraient apporter des conditions plus chaudes et sèches, augmentant les risques d’incendie.

**Conclusion en une phrase** – En 2025, l’Amazonie a connu la plus petite surface brûlée en 40 ans, montrant des progrès dans la lutte contre les incendies tout en soulignant la nécessité de rester vigilants.
articles.personality_title_es: Los incendios en la Amazonía bajan a su nivel más bajo desde 1985 en 2025
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – La selva amazónica, la selva tropical más grande del mundo, sufrió sus peores incendios en más de 20 años en 2024. Estos incendios fueron causados por una mezcla de clima extremo y actividades humanas, quemando grandes áreas.

**Qué pasó** – En 2025, el área quemada en la Amazonía bajó mucho y fue la más pequeña desde 1985, según MapBiomas, un grupo que controla el uso del suelo en Brasil. Cerca de 12,7 millones de hectáreas se quemaron en 2025, frente a 30 millones en 2024. Esta caída del 31 % fue ayudada por un clima más frío llamado La Niña.

**Impacto** – Este cambio es importante porque muestra que es posible reducir los incendios en la Amazonía, que es clave para el clima del planeta. Menos incendios significa menos daño para los animales, plantas y las personas que viven allí. Pero los expertos advierten que esta mejora podría no durar, especialmente si vuelve un clima más cálido.

**Próximo paso** – Científicos y grupos ambientales seguirán observando la Amazonía para ver si la reducción de incendios continúa. También quieren mantener los esfuerzos para prevenir incendios, ya que patrones climáticos como El Niño podrían traer condiciones más secas y calurosas que aumentan el riesgo.

**Conclusión en una frase** – En 2025, la Amazonía tuvo la menor superficie quemada en 40 años, mostrando avances en la lucha contra los incendios pero señalando la necesidad de seguir atentos.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm painting of a vast Amazon rainforest landscape showing vibrant green trees with a clear, flowing river winding through, where in the background gentle blue-gray clouds symbolize the cooling La Niña effect, and subtle glowing embers fading away into the mist represent the sharp decline of forest fires, all rendered in natural, earthy tones without any human figures.

articles.title: In Cuba’s fuel crisis, solar tricycles took the streets
articles.summary:
BY THE OPTIMIST DAILY EDITORIAL TEAM Cuba has long been known for its vintage cars — the American ones that kept rolling through decades of embargo and scarcity, repaired with ingenuity and improvised parts. That ingenuity hasn’t gone anywhere. What it’s being directed at now is something else entirely. The country is in the middle […]
The post In Cuba’s fuel crisis, solar tricycles took the streets first appeared on The Optimist Daily: Making Solutions the News.
articles.content:
BY THE OPTIMIST DAILY EDITORIAL TEAM

Cuba has long been known for its vintage cars — the American ones that kept rolling through decades of embargo and scarcity, repaired with ingenuity and improvised parts. That ingenuity hasn’t gone anywhere. What it’s being directed at now is something else entirely.

The country is in the middle of its worst fuel crisis in years. Oil tanker arrivals dropped from about eight a month to one since January, when the U.S. threatened tariffs on countries selling petroleum to Cuba. The island already produces only about 40 percent of the fuel it needs. Rolling blackouts have worsened, bus routes have been cut, and food and medicine shortages have deepened across the country. What has filled the gap, on street after street in Havana, is the electric tricycle.

A new kind of vehicle for a new kind of crisis

Most of the tricycles are Chinese-made, from brands like Zonsen and Jinpeng, and cost between $2,000 and $4,000. They typically run on gel or lithium batteries and are bought in countries like Panama before being shipped to the island by relatives or importers. Some are assembled in Cuba from parts, under an arrangement with China through the Vedca brand.

For a country where state workers earn around $10 a month, and private-sector employees make roughly $40, that price is far out of reach for most. But a secondary economy has grown up around them. Some Cubans sold their old gasoline-powered cars to buy tricycles. Others received them from relatives abroad, where they are generally cheaper. Some small-business owners invested their profits in them, expecting the vehicles to pay for themselves.

They are. The tricycles now serve fixed bus routes that have disappeared, carry produce and goods to market, and collect garbage in some Havana neighborhoods. Passengers can flag them down for rides costing about 500 Cuban pesos, less than a dollar, which is a real expense in a country where monthly wages sit between $10 and $40. But it beats walking.

“If you can pay for it, you just take it; otherwise you can’t go anywhere,” said Berta Ferrer, 52, a store clerk in central Havana who makes the trip four days a week.

The solar adaptation

What’s more interesting than the tricycles themselves is what some owners have been doing with them.

Many have installed solar panels on the awnings over the passenger seats, allowing the vehicles to recharge while parked or moving rather than waiting for grid power that may or may not arrive. The modification costs about $500, but owners say it pays off fast given how unreliable the grid is.

“There are so many tricycles in Havana that you can’t spend 10 minutes on a street without seeing countless numbers of them pass by,” said Carlos Álvarez, 29, an engineer who runs a workshop specializing in electric vehicles. He was fitting a solar panel mounting bracket onto one of them at the time.

Cuba has been doing this with everything for a long time. Get what you can, adapt it to the conditions you have, and keep it running. What once applied to 1950s Buicks and Chevrolets now applies to a Chinese electric tricycle with a panel on the roof.

Not going anywhere

For a lot of owners, these are no longer emergency measures. “I think this is here to stay,” said Ricardo Quintero, an engineer who uses his tricycle to haul produce to the vegetable stand he runs with his family.

The routes, repair shops, and solar modifications built up around these vehicles would not disappear quickly even if fuel became available again. The streets have reorganized around them.

“This is how people get around now,” said Liecer de la Cruz, 40, who owns one of the vehicles. The old-timers that were still trailing black smoke through Havana streets just a year ago have largely disappeared. What replaced them runs on sunlight and moves without smoke. It’s slower. It’s already how the city works.

Did this solution stand out? Share it with a friend or support our mission by becoming an Emissary.
articles.source: The Optimist Daily: Making Solutions the News
articles.reasoning: The article describes how Cuba, facing a severe fuel crisis, has innovatively adopted solar-powered electric tricycles as a sustainable transportation solution. This shift reduces reliance on scarce fossil fuels, lowers pollution, and creates new economic opportunities for many citizens, demonstrating significant positive environmental and social impact with broad relevance.
articles.personality_title: Cuba turns to solar-powered tricycles amid fuel shortage
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Cuba is facing its worst fuel shortage in years. The country receives far less oil than before, causing blackouts, fewer buses, and shortages of food and medicine.

**What happened** – To deal with this, many people in Havana started using electric tricycles powered by batteries. These tricycles are mostly made in China and cost between $2,000 and $4,000. Because electricity is not always reliable, some owners added solar panels on the tricycle roofs to recharge while moving or parked.

**Impact** – These tricycles now fill in for buses and taxis, helping people get around and businesses move goods. Even though rides cost less than a dollar, many rely on them instead of walking long distances. The solar panels make the vehicles more independent from the unstable power grid. This shows how Cubans are creatively adapting to hard times with clean energy.

**What's next step** – The tricycle system has become part of daily life in Havana. Repair shops and routes have grown around them, so even if fuel returns, these vehicles will likely stay. More solar upgrades and local assembly could help make them even more affordable and reliable.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Faced with fuel shortages, Cubans have embraced solar-powered electric tricycles to keep their city moving in a clean, creative way.
articles.personality_title_fr: Cuba adopte les tricycles solaires face à la pénurie de carburant
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Cuba connaît sa pire pénurie de carburant depuis des années. Le pays reçoit beaucoup moins de pétrole qu'avant, ce qui provoque des coupures d'électricité, moins de bus et des pénuries de nourriture et de médicaments.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Pour faire face à cette situation, de nombreuses personnes à La Havane ont commencé à utiliser des tricycles électriques alimentés par des batteries. Ces tricycles sont principalement fabriqués en Chine et coûtent entre 2 000 et 4 000 dollars. Comme l'électricité est parfois instable, certains propriétaires ont ajouté des panneaux solaires sur le toit des tricycles pour les recharger en roulant ou à l'arrêt.

**Impact** – Ces tricycles remplacent désormais les bus et les taxis, aidant les gens à se déplacer et les commerçants à transporter leurs marchandises. Même si les trajets coûtent moins d'un dollar, beaucoup les utilisent plutôt que de marcher longtemps. Les panneaux solaires rendent les véhicules moins dépendants du réseau électrique instable. Cela montre comment les Cubains s'adaptent de façon créative aux difficultés avec une énergie propre.

**Prochaine étape** – Le système de tricycles fait désormais partie de la vie quotidienne à La Havane. Des ateliers de réparation et des itinéraires se sont développés autour d'eux, donc même si le carburant revient, ces véhicules resteront probablement. Plus d'améliorations solaires et d'assemblage local pourraient les rendre encore plus abordables et fiables.

**Phrase clé** – Face à la pénurie de carburant, les Cubains ont adopté les tricycles électriques solaires pour faire avancer leur ville de manière propre et inventive.
articles.personality_title_es: Cuba adopta triciclos solares ante la escasez de combustible
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Cuba enfrenta su peor crisis de combustible en años. El país recibe mucho menos petróleo que antes, lo que causa apagones, menos autobuses y escasez de alimentos y medicinas.

**Qué pasó** – Para enfrentar esto, muchas personas en La Habana comenzaron a usar triciclos eléctricos que funcionan con baterías. Estos triciclos son en su mayoría fabricados en China y cuestan entre 2,000 y 4,000 dólares. Debido a que la electricidad no siempre es confiable, algunos dueños les instalaron paneles solares en el techo para recargarlos mientras se mueven o están estacionados.

**Impacto** – Estos triciclos ahora reemplazan a los autobuses y taxis, ayudando a las personas a desplazarse y a los negocios a transportar productos. Aunque los viajes cuestan menos de un dólar, muchos dependen de ellos en lugar de caminar largas distancias. Los paneles solares hacen que los vehículos sean menos dependientes de la red eléctrica inestable. Esto muestra cómo los cubanos se adaptan creativamente a tiempos difíciles con energía limpia.

**Próximo paso** – El sistema de triciclos se ha vuelto parte de la vida diaria en La Habana. Talleres de reparación y rutas han crecido alrededor de ellos, por lo que aunque el combustible regrese, estos vehículos probablemente se quedarán. Más mejoras solares y ensamblaje local podrían hacerlos aún más accesibles y confiables.

**Frase clave** – Frente a la escasez de combustible, los cubanos han adoptado triciclos eléctricos solares para mantener su ciudad en movimiento de manera limpia y creativa.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of a vibrant Havana street filled with colorful electric tricycles topped with solar panels, gently glowing under soft sunlight, with their silhouettes carrying baskets of fresh produce and passengers, set against the backdrop of classic pastel buildings and a clear sky, using natural, muted tones to evoke resilience and hopeful adaptation.
